entries.definition:
كتاب الجيم

جزأ - جزي

(جزي) الجيم والزاء والياء: قيام الشيء مَقامَ غيره ومكافأتُه إياه. يقال جَزَيت فلاناً أجزِيه جزاءً، وجازيتُه مجازاةً. وهذا رجل جازِيكَ مِنْ رجل،

j–z–y recompense, to repay, to reward, to punish for bad deeds, judgement; to stand in for; taxation, tribute. Of this root, six forms occur 118 times in the Qurʾan: جَزَىٰ jazā 50 times; تُجْزَىٰ tujzā 23 times; نُجَازِي nujāzī once; جَازٍ jāzin once; جَزَاء jazāʾ 42 times and جِزْيَة jizyatun once.
جَزَىٰ jazā i I [v. trans.] 1 [with prep. بِـ] to recompense, to reward/punish (53:31) لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا بِالْحُسْنَىٰ that He will repay those who do evil for what they did and reward with what is best those who do good 2 [with prep. عَنْ] to stand in for, to take the place of, to undertake responsibility for

163
entries.definition_english:
j–z–y recompense, to repay, to reward, to punish for bad deeds, judgement; to stand in for; taxation, tribute. Of this root, six forms occur 118 times in the Qurʾan: جَزَىٰ jazā 50 times; تُجْزَىٰ tujzā 23 times; نُجَازِي nujāzī once; جَازٍ jāzin once; جَزَاء jazāʾ 42 times and جِزْيَة jizyatun once.
جَزَىٰ jazā i I [v. trans.] 1 [with prep. بِـ] to recompense, to reward/punish (53:31) لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا بِالْحُسْنَىٰ that He will repay those who do evil for what they did and reward with what is best those who do good 2 [with prep. عَنْ] to stand in for, to take the place of, to undertake responsibility for

163

entries.definition:
(31:33) وَاخْشَوْا يَوْمًا لَا يَجْزِي وَالِدٌ عَنْ وَلَدِهِ and fear a day when no parent will take the place of their child II [v., doubly transitive] to compensate, to reward, to pay someone for something (28:25) قَالَتْ إِنَّ أَبِي يَدْعُوكَ لِيَجْزِيَكَ أَجْرَ مَا سَقَيْتَ لَنَا she said, ‘My father invites you so that he may pay you the wages for having watered [our flock] for us.’
تُجْزَىٰ tujzā [pass. v.] to be rewarded/punished, to be recompensed, to be paid (20:15) إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخْفِيهَا لِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا تَسْعَىٰ the Hour is coming; I almost keep it hidden, so that each soul be rewarded for what it does.

نُجَازِي nujāzī [imperf. of v. III جَازَىٰ jāzā trans.] to repay a bad deed in particular, to recompense, to punish (34:17) ذَٰلِكَ جَزَيْنَاهُمْ بِمَا كَفَرُوا وَهَلْ نُجَازِي إِلَّا الْكَفُورَ in this way We recompensed them for their ingratitude—do We ever punish anyone but the ungrateful!

جَازٍ jāzin [act. part. with عَنْ] one who stands in for another, one who undertakes to do something instead of another (31:33) وَلَا مَوْلُودٌ هُوَ جَازٍ عَنْ وَالِدِهِ شَيْئًا nor will a child take the place of his parent for anything.

جَزَاء jazāʾ [n./v. n.] 1 reward (20:76) وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ مَنْ تَزَكَّىٰ such is the reward of those who purify themselves 2 retribution (9:82) فَلْيَضْحَكُوا قَلِيلًا وَلْيَبْكُوا كَثِيرًا جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ let them laugh a little; they will weep a lot as a retribution for what they have been doing 3 penalty, requital (5:95) وَمَنْ قَتَلَهُ مِنْكُمْ مُتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ whoever from among you kills it [the hunted animal] intentionally the penalty for it is [an offering of] the equivalent in domestic animals of that which he has killed.

جِزْيَة jizyatun [n.] payment in return; [jur.] head tax collected in return for receiving the protection of the Muslim state with all the accruing benefits, as well as exemption from military service and from such taxes required of Muslims as Zakāt. This tax, from which members of the clergy were exempted, was levied only on able-bodied free males who could afford it. The amount generally varied between one and two dinars per year and was determined in accordance with the means of each individual (9:29) قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لَا

164

This tax, from which members of the clergy were exempted, was levied only on able-bodied free males who could afford it.
